L1.description: A sandbox escape vulnerability exists where certain foreign function interface (FFI) operations in the runtime fail to enforce required permission and stability checks. An attacker controlling code within a sandboxed execution environment can directly invoke low-level native function pointer calls and memory read operations, bypassing the runtime's permission model entirely. This allows arbitrary code execution even when the dangerous capability has not been explicitly granted by the user.

ground_truth.reason: FFI operations for calling native function pointers and reading memory did not enforce permission or stability checks, allowing sandboxed code to bypass the runtime's security model and execute arbitrary native code.
